message:
perf(record): inline serial_byte_and_payload_len + encode_serialized_value

Two small `#[inline]` annotations to follow up on the stack-allocated
record serialization fast path. Both helpers are called once per
column on the hot path, and both are tiny — the function call
overhead is meaningful relative to the work each one does.

crates/fsqlite-types/src/record.rs:
- `PrecomputedSerialTypeKind::serial_byte_and_payload_len` gets
  `#[inline]`. The body is a `match` over a small enum returning a
  short `(u8, usize)` tuple per arm; with `#[inline]` the compiler
  can fold the dispatch into the caller's loop and constant-fold
  the per-kind cases when the kind is known statically.
- `encode_serialized_value` gets `#[inline(always)]`. It's a
  per-column dispatch over `SqliteValue`'s variants writing into a
  pre-sized `&mut [u8]`. Forcing it inline lets the caller — which
  is now the stack-allocated fast path — keep the buf slice and
  length in registers across the whole serialization loop.

No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
